textPlain: How do you people even keep up with this? I'm going back to cybersecurity after trying DevOps for a year, it's not for me. I miss my sysadmin days, things were simple back then and worked. Maybe I'm just getting old and my cognitive abilities are declining. It seems to me that the current tech scene doesn't reward simple.
This is required steps but the timing plan is bad. Looks like a google product closing. Let people time to move out, 6 month is not enough.
Traefik has an Nginx compatibility for annotations as well to make it easy to switch.

Reading few blogs and forums about it today - people talking about switching to Gateway API (from "legacy" Ingress).And I do not understand it:1. Ingress still works, it's not deprecated.2. There a lot of controllers, which supports both: Gateway API and Ingress (for example Traefik)So, how Ingress Nginx retiring related / affects switch to Gateway API?
RIP, end of an era. Thank you everyone who worked on this, it was an extraordinarily useful and reliable project.
It's in beta, but HAProxy has a gateway product:https://www.haproxy.com/blog/announcing-haproxy-unified-gate...
Does anyone know good resources on how to migrate and which gateway controllers are suitable replacements?Ingresses with custom nginx attributes might be tricky to migrate.
Another triumph for open source: popular project probably used by many megacorps only propped up by the weekend charity of a couple unpaid suckers over the years.

Ingress nginx was the default ingress for pretty much the entire life of k8s. F5 bought nginx and made nginx ingress, which I've never met a user of.Sad to see such a core component die, but I guess now everyone has to migrate to gateways.
It wasn't the most loved part of k8s, to say the least.
Great, another deprecation to address in my EKS clusters :(
Why would you kill a thing that works so well, is so flexible, and does not have an equal yet?I do not understand.

It's exactly why taking a trip through the ops/infra side is so important for people - you learn why LTS-style engineering is so important. You learn to pick technologies that are stable, reliable, well-supported by a large-enough people who are conservative in their approach, for anything foundational, because the alternative is migration pain again and again.

> It seems to me that the current tech scene doesn't reward simple.A deal with the devil was made. The C suite gets to tell a story that k8s practices let you suck every penny out of the compute you already paid for. Modern devs get to do constant busy work adding complexity everywhere, creating job security and opportunities to use fun new toys. "Here's how we're using AI to right size our pods! Never mind the actual costs and reliability compared to traditional infrastructure, we only ever need to talk about the happy path/best case scenarios."
It doesn't but Kubernetes team was kind of like "Hey, while you are switching, maybe switch away from Ingress API?"
Love haproxy but if we’re shilling projects istio is superior. Multi cluster, hbone, ambient.

I've been using Envoy Gateway in my homelab and have found it to be good for my modest needs (single node k3s cluster running on an old PC). I needed to configure the underlying EnvoyProxy so that it would listen on specific IPs provided by MetalLB, and their docs were good enough to find my way through that.https://gateway.envoyproxy.io/
There are no maintainers. It was maintained by one engineer for years, he stepped down, and F5 (who bought nginx) don't want to contribute since they have a competitor.
